quality_certificate_date: Extract the certificate issue date. Labels: 'Date of Issue' / 'DATE' / 'Date'. Location varies by template — may be in the top header column, near the bottom-right of the form, or inline near a place of issue. Examples: '16 APR. 2025', '26.03.2025', '23/01/2026'. May be absent in some templates — if not present, leave empty. DO NOT confuse with date_of_production (a per-item field) or with expiry date. Keep the exact format from the document and add to quality_certificate_date.
producer: Identify the producer/processing establishment. Sources in priority order: (1) explicit field labeled 'Producer' / 'Plant number' / 'NAME AND ADDRESS OF PROCESSING ESTABLISHMENT' / 'NAME AND ADDRESS OF PROCESSING PLANT' (often paired with a registration number); (2) MAY also appear as 'FACTORY EU APPROVAL NUMBER' or 'FACTORY NO' alongside a producer name; (3) FALLBACK: when no explicit producer label exists, use the company name from the document HEADER/title block (logo + company name at top). Examples: 'Time Seafood (Dalian) Co.,Ltd 2100/02847', 'SALMONES ANTARTICA S.A. 10735', 'WEIHAI TRANSPORT AQUATIC FOOD CO.,LTD'. Keep only the name (address can be omitted) and add to producer.
container_no: Extract container number(s). Format: 4 letters + 7 digits (examples: 'CRXU6954811', 'MSGU9063090', 'PONU4832160'). Sources vary: explicit field 'Container No.' / 'CONTAINER N°' / 'Container and Seals Number', or inside REMARKS as 'CONTAINER AND SEAL NO.: <container>/<seal>' (in such combined entries the first part is the container, the second is the seal). May be absent when transport is by truck. If multiple, join with '; '. Add to container_no.
vessel: Extract the vessel/ship name only when transport is by sea. Labels: 'Means of Conveyance' / 'MEANS OF TRANSPORT' / 'Name of Vessel'. Examples: 'BY VESSEL', 'LEXA MAERSK 935N'. If transport is BY TRUCK ('TRUCK PLATE NUMBER 61 K 8214 / 61 AAL 959'), leave vessel EMPTY — DO NOT confuse with truck plate numbers. Add the vessel name and voyage number in one line if shown.
veterinary_seal: Extract veterinary/health seal number(s) only when explicitly labeled as veterinary/health seal, OR when shown as part of 'CONTAINER AND SEAL NO.: <container>/<seal>' in REMARKS (in that combined entry the seal is the second part, e.g., 'FX41279555'). DO NOT confuse with linear_seal (which is the linear/general seal) or with veterinary_certificate_no (which is the document identifier). If only a generic 'SEAL: ...' is shown without a veterinary label and no combined REMARKS entry, treat it as a linear seal and leave veterinary_seal empty.
linear_seal: Extract linear seal number(s). Labels: 'Seal' / 'SEAL' / 'Container and Seals Number'. Located in dedicated fields or in REMARKS section. If multiple, join with '; '. DO NOT confuse with veterinary_seal. Add to linear_seal.
products: The 'products' block is expandable: create product_1, product_2, ... for each goods position/table row in the document and keep order. For each product_i fill all available fields. If an attribute is given once in a shared block (e.g., one Latin species for all lines), duplicate it in the relevant product_i. In token_refs include references to the source fragments (cells/rows) used.
products[*].name_product: Extract the commercial name. Labels: 'Description of Goods' / 'DESCRIPTION OF THE GOODS' / 'Product' / 'DESCRIPTION OF GOODS'. Examples: 'FROZEN OILFISH HGT', 'FROZEN RAINBOW TROUT WR IQF', 'Frozen seaweed salad chuka salad', 'FROZEN RAINBOW TROUT HG'. Join line breaks into one line and add to products[i].name_product.
products[*].latin_name: Extract the Latin species name. Most often appears in parentheses directly after the commercial name (e.g., '(LEPIDOCYBIUM FLAVOBRUNNEUM)', '(ONCORHYNCHUS MYKISS)', '(Undaria Pinnatifida)'). Less commonly in a separate field 'Species and Scientific name'. If absent, leave empty. Add to products[i].latin_name.
products[*].size_product: Extract size/grade/pack if present in the goods description (e.g., '0.8-1.2 KG', '10 kg (10 IVP bags*1kg)'). May not be shown — if absent, leave empty. Add to products[i].size_product.
products[*].unit_box: Extract the package type/unit. Labels: 'Number and Type of Packages' / 'package /kg' / 'Type and quantity' / 'Kind of Packing' / part of 'NUMBER OF PACKAGES: 880 CARTONS'. Keep the wording (e.g., 'CARTONS', 'BOXES', 'BAGS', 'IVP bags', 'CASES'). If no explicit unit, leave empty. Add to products[i].unit_box.
products[*].packages: Extract the number of packages. Labels: 'Number of Packages' / 'Quantity/Weight Declared' / 'NO. BOX' / 'cartons'. Examples: '1153 BAGS', '738 BOXES', '2500 cartons', '880 CARTONS'. If a packing type is shown alongside, keep both in one line. Add to products[i].packages.
products[*].gross_weight: Extract gross weight per item if shown. Labels: 'Gross weight' / 'GROSS WEIGHT KGS' / 'GROSS WEIGHT +PL KGS' / 'G.W.'. Often absent in QC documents — if missing, leave empty. Keep number with unit in original format. Add to products[i].gross_weight.
products[*].net_weight: Extract net weight per item. Labels: 'Net weight' / 'NET WEIGHT KGS' / 'TOTAL NET WEIGHT IN KG' / 'Quantity/Weight Declared' / 'net weight/kg'. If a 'Total Net Weight' is shown and per-item net weights are in the table, use the table values for products[i].net_weight and do not duplicate the total. Keep number and unit as in the document. Add to products[i].net_weight.
products[*].date_of_production: Extract production date(s). Labels: 'Production Date' / 'DATE OF PRODUCTION' / 'PRODUCTION DATE'. Location varies — may be a dedicated column, an inline value in product description, or inside a REMARKS section (e.g., 'DATE OF PRODUCTION: 20.02.2025'). May be a single date or a list with batch quantities (e.g., '05-06-2019(1), 23-07-2019(4), ...'). If multiple dates, join with '; ' in one line. DO NOT confuse with quality_certificate_date (issue date) or expiry date. Add to products[i].date_of_production.
